tagline: Staff Frontend Engineer & Design Systems Architect
cover:
I'm excited to apply for the Senior Design Engineer role at iSeatz. With 12+ years building & leading enterprise design systems—including Spectrum at Adobe & PatternFly Elements at Red Hat—I bring deep expertise in bridging the gap between design intent & technical implementation across multi-product platforms.

At Adobe, I led frontend architecture for the Spectrum CSS Design System implementation, serving flagship products such as Photoshop and Express, reaching millions of customers. I migrated documentation & prototyping to Storybook, architected modern build systems to reduce technical debt, & conducted thorough code reviews ensuring WCAG AA compliance & design fidelity across all components. Collaborating closely with design & accessibility experts, I ensured consistent component delivery across diverse product teams—experience directly applicable to iSeatz's multi-tenant, white-label platform.

At Red Hat, I co-founded PatternFly Elements, building a theming architecture that enabled consistent branding across disparate applications—essentially a white-label component system. I drove adoption across Red Hat & partner organizations (Google, IBM, Fidelity, Penn State), engaging designers, developers, & stakeholders to align on shared patterns. This cross-organization collaboration mirrors the kind of systemic thinking iSeatz needs to serve partners like American Express, IHG, & Qantas.

As a W3C CSS Working Group member, I bring standards-level understanding of CSS & web components that informs practical, forward-looking design system decisions. I'm passionate about accessibility, reducing complexity through quality work, & building systems that empower teams to ship consistent experiences at scale.

I'd welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience can help iSeatz's design system serve your partners with consistency, accessibility, & technical excellence.
about: Staff Frontend Engineer with 12+ years building enterprise design systems that serve multiple products & brands with consistency & accessibility. W3C CSS Working Group member with deep expertise in HTML5, CSS3, Sass, TypeScript, Storybook, & WCAG compliance. Proven track record architecting multi-tenant component systems, migrating documentation tooling, & bridging the gap between design intent & technical implementation. Passionate about reducing complexity, driving cross-functional collaboration, & building systemic frontend infrastructure that empowers teams to ship faster.

experience.title: Consultant & Owner
experience.summary: Consulting practice focused on design system architecture & frontend enablement, helping teams build scalable, accessible component systems that maintain design consistency across products.
highlights: Leveraging AI tools (Cursor, Claude) to improve developer workflows & maximize productivity
Publishing detailed guides & documentation that codify design system best practices
Designed & presented workshops on design system best practices & methodologies for frontend engineering teams

experience.title: Staff Design Engineer, Spectrum Design System
experience.summary: Led frontend architecture for Adobe's CSS implementation, serving multiple flagship products (Photoshop, Illustrator, Express). Drove consistent component delivery across diverse product teams while ensuring accessibility & design fidelity—directly bridging design intent & technical implementation.
highlights: Migrated documentation to Storybook, establishing a source of truth for component behavior & visual specifications
Architected modern CSS build system reducing technical debt & enabling consistent theming across Adobe's multi-product ecosystem
Technical leader for web components: conducted thorough code reviews ensuring WCAG AA compliance, keyboard navigation, & high-contrast mode support
Collaborated with design & accessibility experts to translate design specifications into production-ready components
Early member of Spectrum AI Working Group: established Cursor guidelines & built AI-assisted workflow that improved cross-team productivity
Represented Adobe in W3C CSS Working Group, directly influencing web platform standards for component styling
Drove consistent component delivery across distributed product teams in agile environment

experience.title: Principal Front-end Architect, User Experience
experience.summary: Co-founded & led PatternFly Elements, building a white-label-ready web component system with distributed theming architecture. Drove adoption across Red Hat & partner organizations while collaborating on shared standards with major tech companies.
highlights: Co-founded PatternFly Elements: built distributed theming architecture enabling consistent branding across disparate applications—a white-label component system serving multiple organizations
Cross-organization collaboration with Google, IBM, Fidelity, & Penn State: aligned on shared patterns & drove standardization across enterprise applications
Drove component adoption by engaging designers, developers, & stakeholders to prioritize high-impact features based on user needs & product requirements
Expert-level Sass developer: optimized CSS architecture for maintainability, theming flexibility, & cross-browser consistency
Built semantic, WCAG AA compliant components with keyboard navigation & high-contrast mode as requirements for all assets
Active CSS Working Group & web components standards contributor: represented team priorities & influenced platform direction
Community management: facilitated contributions through documentation, code review, & fostering healthy contributor culture across distributed teams
